findings.detail: All 7 inputs validated before computation: win_probability [0,1], win_loss_ratio > 0, user_conviction_discount [0,1], volatility_percentile [0,1], correlation_to_portfolio [-1,1], portfolio_pct_limit (0,1]. Each raises ValueError with descriptive message on invalid input.

Kelly formula: (1-W)/R — win_loss_ratio validated > 0 before use. No other divisions by dynamic values. Correlation discount: std_i > 0 and std_j > 0 checks before division (but those are in ranking_engine, not here).

All inputs validated for valid ranges. User conviction can only DISCOUNT win probability (multiplied, never added). Raw Kelly clamped to [0, 1] after computation. Volatility adjustment clamped to [0.3, 1.2]. Correlation discount clamped to [0.2, 1.0]. Hard cap enforced.
Pure mathematical function. No LLM calls, no network I/O, no file I/O, no datetime calls. Zero side effects.

User conviction can only REDUCE win probability (adjusted_prob = win_probability * user_conviction_discount with discount in [0,1]). This is a critical safety constraint — prevents users from inflating their confidence to justify larger positions.
